title: Pinalia pachystachya
taxonomy: Pinalia pachystachya is classified within the family Orchidaceae. This family is commonly known as the orchid family and is the largest family of flowering plants. Pinalia pachystachya is further classified within the subfamily Epidendroideae. Within this subfamily, Pinalia pachystachya is part of the tribe Vandeae. The genus Pinalia is a genus of orchids native to tropical regions of Asia.

intro: Pinalia pachystachya, also known as the dwarf orchid, is a type of epiphytic orchid native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, fever, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Pinalia pachystachya include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Pinalia pachystachya is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or infusion, and is commonly used to make cough syrups, teas, and other remedies for respiratory and digestive ailments.
